title: Semi-Automatic 3D Cryo-ET Fiber Analysis
author: Kerkhoff, Yannic
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This protocol provides a semi-automatic workflow for analyzing 3D cryo-electron tomography (cryo-ET) data, specifically designed for fiber structures. The tool facilitates the segmentation, skeletonization, and advanced analysis of fiber networks in noisy datasets. Key features include background correction, noise reduction via 3D Gaussian blur, thresholding for fiber segmentation, and particle analysis to filter objects based on size. The protocol also supports visualization through maximum intensity projections and 3D reconstructions, along with post-processing steps such as k-means clustering to separate artifacts from valid fiber structures. The tool is implemented as a macro for Fiji, ensuring reproducibility through detailed logging of processing parameters.
